input: Lighthouse JSON summary for a Next.js e-commerce product page: Performance score 41. LCP: 7.2s (image element, above-fold hero, loaded from CDN but not preloaded). CLS: 0.28 (layout shift traced to a late-loading font causing text reflow + an ad slot with no reserved dimensions). INP: 340ms (traced to a React hydration event on a 280kb client bundle that includes a full date-picker library used only in checkout). FCP: 2.1s. TTFB: 380ms.
rubric: identifies LCP root cause (missing preload, not CDN latency)
identifies CLS as two distinct sources: font swap + unsized ad slot
identifies INP as hydration cost not layout thrash — distinguishes the two
fix list is prioritized by score impact, not alphabetical
fix list is actionable — each item names the specific element, resource, or code change required

input: Page description: A React SPA blog. FCP: 4.8s. LCP: 6.1s (the LCP element is an H1 text node, not an image). INP: 210ms. CLS: 0.04. The JS bundle is 1.1MB uncompressed, served without code splitting. The page calls three third-party analytics scripts in <head> with no async/defer. Web fonts are loaded via Google Fonts @import in a CSS file (render-blocking). Server renders nothing — full client-side render.
rubric: identifies LCP as text node and explains why SSR or prerendering would help
identifies render-blocking third-party scripts as FCP contributor
identifies Google Fonts @import as render-blocking (vs. preconnect + link rel=preload approach)
distinguishes layout thrash (not present here) from hydration/parsing cost (the actual issue)
prioritized fix list covers bundle splitting, async scripts, and font loading separately

input: Lighthouse JSON summary for a Shopify storefront: Performance score 55. LCP: 4.9s (product hero image, 680kb uncompressed JPEG, no WebP variant, served from Shopify CDN). CLS: 0.19 (carousel component shifts content on load; image dimensions not specified in HTML). INP: 180ms (theme JS registers 14 scroll event listeners without passive flag, causing jank during scroll). TBT: 890ms (three large vendor scripts: Klaviyo 210kb, Yotpo 180kb, Lucky Orange 140kb).
rubric: identifies LCP as image size/format issue, not CDN latency
identifies CLS as missing dimension attributes on carousel images
identifies INP as non-passive scroll listeners (layout thrash pattern) — not hydration
distinguishes layout thrash (scroll listeners) from hydration cost
prioritized fix list addresses image format, dimension attributes, passive listeners, and third-party script deferral separately

input: Page description: A WordPress marketing site with Elementor page builder. LCP: 5.6s (above-fold background image set via CSS background-image property, not an tag — cannot be preloaded by the browser scanner). CLS: 0.31 (sticky header animates height on scroll; no transform used, forces layout recalc each frame). INP: 290ms (Elementor loads 18 CSS files and 12 JS files; main thread blocked during parsing for 1.1s). TTFB: 190ms. Server response is fast.
rubric: identifies LCP root cause as CSS background-image not being preload-scannable
identifies CLS as layout-recalc from non-transform animation (height change)
identifies INP as main-thread parse blocking from asset count, not a single large bundle
fix list distinguishes quick wins (preload hint via JS, transform:translateY fix) from structural changes (asset consolidation)
does not recommend migrating off Elementor as the primary fix — stays within the constraint

input: Lighthouse JSON summary for a Vue 3 dashboard SaaS: Performance score 62 on desktop, 29 on mobile. LCP: 3.1s desktop / 8.4s mobile (same element: a data table rendered after two sequential API calls that must resolve before render). CLS: 0.02. INP: 520ms mobile only (traced to a virtualized list component that re-renders the full visible window on every scroll tick; no debounce or RAF scheduling). TBT: 1.2s mobile (single 420kb vendor chunk: ag-Grid full build, only 30% of features used).
rubric: identifies LCP as sequential API waterfall blocking render (not a resource issue)
identifies INP as scroll-handler re-render without RAF — distinguishes from layout thrash
notes the desktop/mobile performance gap and explains why mobile is disproportionately affected
identifies ag-Grid bundle as over-included (tree-shaking opportunity)
prioritized fix list addresses API parallelization, RAF scheduling, and bundle reduction separately

input: Page description: A statically generated Astro site for a SaaS marketing page. Performance score 91. LCP: 1.8s. CLS: 0.22 (a cookie consent banner injects above content after hydration, pushing the hero section down by ~80px with no reserved space). INP: 95ms. One issue flagged: a <video autoplay loop muted> element in the hero is 14MB and has no width/height attributes. The video does not affect LCP because the LCP element is the H1 above it, but it increases TBT on slow connections by delaying interactive.
rubric: correctly identifies the cookie banner as the sole CLS source
correctly identifies that the video does NOT cause the LCP issue (LCP is the H1)
explains the video's actual impact: TBT and TTI on slow connections, not LCP or CLS
fix list is minimal — does not over-engineer a 91-score page
at least one fix addresses reserving space for the consent banner without breaking layout
